channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: 'We pay our rates': Call for island to introduce public bus
item.description: Macleay Island residents say the community has a "very high percentage" of people who are elderly and have a disability, who would benefit from a bus service.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Richards

media:group.media:description: Residents call the incline to the ferry "Angina Hill", with many people feeling unwell on it.

item.title: Community 'devastated' by plans for gas-powered data centres
item.description: More than 200 people voice their opposition to plans to build one of the country’s largest gas-fired power plants in the NSW Southern Highlands to provide energy for data centres.
item.dc:creator: Penny Burfitt

media:group.media:description: Community members gathered to protest the planned gas-powered data centre campus on Friday.

item.title: Push to find resting places for remains found in desecrated graves
item.description: Nine piles of dirt containing up to 5,000 human remains from dug-up graves have been dumped at the rear of a regional Victorian cemetery. A team of archaeologists is helping to find a home for the disturbed remains.
item.dc:creator: Nate Woodall

media:group.media:description: Deb Tranter estimates up to 5,000 graves had been desecrated.

item.title: 'Intolerable': When $768m bridge was being designed, engineers raised a red flag
item.description: There are fresh concerns over the structural integrity of Hobart's new Bridgewater Bridge, which one expert who worked on the project suggested could eventually lead to a need to reduce traffic flow or speed limits.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Rojahn

media:group.media:description: A close-up of the monitoring device.

item.title: What we know about the remaining families returning from Syria
item.description: The last cohort of ISIS-linked Australians living in a notorious Syrian camp are expected to return back into Australia in the coming hours. Here's what we know about them.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Doran

media:group.media:description: A bus carrying Australian women and children left the al-Roj camp last week.

item.title: The Enhanced Games flattered to deceive, but that's probably the point
item.description: Despite the drugs and the super suits, the Enhanced Games delivered little more than a smattering of PBs and a single world fastest time for the hefty price of an athlete's hard-earned credibility.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Was the Enhanced Games a success? Probably depends on who you ask.

item.title: NACC chief Brereton to face Senate grilling after shock resignation
item.description: National Anti-Corruption Commissioner Paul Brereton will face a grilling by senators at estimates hearings today following his shock resignation on Monday, amid calls for a wider reset of the troubled agency.
item.dc:creator: Jake Evans

media:group.media:description: Paul Brereton has announced his resignation from the National Anti-Corruption Commission.

item.title: From 'the Reverend' to the 'Big Freeze', Neale Daniher never stopped leading
item.description: Neale Daniher showed leadership on and off the field throughout his life, from club captaincy to coaching and as Australian of the Year — but no more so when facing the challenge of an MND diagnosis.
item.dc:creator: Andrew McGarry

media:group.media:description: Neale Daniher's popularity with people never faded, particularly with Melbourne fans.

item.title: Xi served Putin and Trump Peking duck. Albanese missed out
item.description: Xi Jinping has learned that every ingredient placed on a state table is a word in a diplomatic sentence.
item.dc:creator: Bang Xiao

media:group.media:description: Xi Jinping has learned that every ingredient placed on a state table is a word in a diplomatic sentence.

item.title: Labor to ignore call for pause on capital gains tax changes
item.description: The government is pushing ahead with capital gains tax changes while consulting on limited carve-outs.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says Labor will revisit implementation after introducing it to Senate.

item.title: Leaked documents reveal doubts over BHP's ability to hit net zero
item.description: The mining giant's delay in the rollout of renewable energy and a fleet of electric trucks and trains in the Pilbara has raised doubts inside the company about it meeting its ambitious climate goal.
item.dc:creator: Angus Grigg, Alex McDonald, and Jade Toomey

media:group.media:description: A BHP mining truck driving in a supplied video from the mining company.

item.title: Rate hikes and tax changes have broken the back of the latest property boom
item.description: Early signs of an Australian housing market correction are now clearly visible, and at the heart of the recent property market weakness is less vendor power.
item.dc:creator: David Taylor

media:group.media:description: Interest rate hikes and the federal budget are affecting the property market.

item.title: Flexible work broader than just working from home
item.description: A bill amending the Fair Work Act 2009 to include the right to work from home is too narrow in its focus and could lead to discrimination, according to submissions in a federal inquiry.
item.dc:creator: Ahmed Yussuf

media:group.media:description: More than a third of Australians work from home with about half of them doing so between one and four days a week.

item.title: Missing man's ex-girlfriend feared being 'jumped'
item.description: The former girlfriend of missing Queensland man Jack McLennan says she held fears for her safety because of rumours surrounding his disappearance.
item.dc:creator: Robert Burgin

media:group.media:description: Jack McLennan had been in a relationship with Carlee Beetham for 18 months before his disappearance.

item.title: The company with rare licence to legally manufacture MDMA, magic mushrooms
item.description: Inside these vaults lies millions of dollars worth of medicine used in controversial therapies.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller and Eric Hao Zheng

item.title: How and how not to use AI for help with your finances
item.description: "Hallucinations", privacy and other factors to consider if you’re turning to artificial intelligence financial advice.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chisholm

media:group.media:description: Experts recommend being cautious and critical if you are asking AI chatbots for money advice.

item.title: In 2024, Richard Gadd gave us Baby Reindeer. He follows it up with Half Man
item.description: After Baby Reindeer — about a comedian and his stalker — became a surprise hit, Richard Gadd ignored the pressure to replicate his success, making the show he couldn't stop thinking about.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Story

media:group.media:description: Richard Gadd won Emmys for producing, starring in and writing Baby Reindeer, his first TV show, and returns to the screen with Half Man.

item.title: Flag quiz: Which country has the most purple on its flag?
item.description: What's the name of the solo star on the Australian flag? And can you tell the difference between the Russian and Dutch flags?

media:group.media:description: The Australian flag outside Parliament House in Canberra.

item.title: Thai cave rescuers work to free seven people trapped in flooded Laos cave
item.description: Seven villagers entered the cave in central Laos five days ago looking for gold when heavy rain caused flash flooding, trapping them inside.

media:group.media:description: Rescuers work to reach seven people who are trapped inside a cave in central Laos.

item.title: Ebola outbreak outpacing health response, WHO chief says
item.description: It comes as health facilities in some of the worst-hit regions are attacked amid ongoing violence.

media:group.media:description: Health authorities are racing to keep the outbreak from spreading further, but are being hampered by regional instability.

item.title: Trump demands Arab nations recognise Israel as part of Iran war deal
item.description: Donald Trump has boldly tied negotiations to end his war with Iran to a swathe of Arab nations formally establishing diplomatic ties with Israel.

media:group.media:description: Trump has called on Arab nations to sign the Abraham Accords, normalising relations with Israel.

item.title: More than 20 hospitalised after substance sprayed in Tokyo mall
item.description: The shopping centre, located in the touristy and upmarket shopping district of Ginza, has been blocked off following the incident.

media:group.media:description: Emergency services examine the scene after a person allegedly sprayed a substance in a Tokyo shopping centre.

item.title: Iran says 'no tolls' on Strait of Hormuz, but warns everything has a cost
item.description: While on assignment inside Iran, our reporter asked a foreign ministry official about the regime's plan for the Strait of Hormuz. The spokesperson responded that "there is no toll" but also signalled crossing the strait will not be entirely free.
item.dc:creator: Emily Clark and Karishma Vyas

media:group.media:description: From inside Tehran, the ABC asked the regime if it intended to charge tolls for ships wanting to cross the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: BHP talked up its green plans for WA. Leaked documents show it then shelved them
item.description: Hundreds of pages of internal BHP documents reveal how the company sold itself as a climate leader while internally finding reasons to put off action in the Pilbara.
item.dc:creator: Angus Grigg, Alex McDonald, and Jade Toomey

media:group.media:description: A graphic showing a mining truck, a BHP train, solar and wind farms and excerpts from internal documents leaked to Four Corners and Guardian Australia.

item.title: Australian astronaut offered place on breakthrough space flight
item.description: The European Space Agency has formally extended the offer for Australia's Katherine Bennell-Pegg to join a mission that would take her to the International Space Station, but the place is not guaranteed.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas R Ward and Andrew Williams

media:group.media:description: Katherine Bennell-Pegg was named 2026 Australian of the Year after being the first Australian woman receive astronaut wings.
